channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Dark Fantasy Open-World RPG The Blood of Dawnwalker Gets a Reveal Stream, With Launch Date, Next Week
item.content:encoded:
The Blood of Dawnwalker, Rebel Wolves’ upcoming open-world vampire action RPG, is getting a special “Road to Launch” event next week that will feature new details, fresh gameplay, a new story trailer, system specs and a launch window.

The livestream event is happening on April 28th at 9:00 a.m. Pacific time on the game’s YouTube and Twitch channels. The team has already confirmed that the launch is set for sometime this year, and that launch date looks to be revealed next week. This is going to be a milestone reveal stream.

The Blood of Dawnwalker is set to be the first chapter of an original multi-part dark fantasy saga.

You play Coen, a man who, in 14th century Europe, has become a Dawnwalker.  this means he's not human, but nor is he a full vampire. He benefits and is weakened by both of these worlds, and the team has already said he'll be able to forge his own path and play your decisions will absolutely matter. 

You can watch the new age-restricted teaser here.

Coen can choose to fight to save his family from Brencis, the vampire lord that has taken to ruling Vale Sangora, or he can choose other paths. It will be up to you to guide Coen the way you want to and discover exactly what the developers of Rebel Wolves (a Polish studio full of devs with RPG experience, including Game Director Konrad Tomaszkiewicz, who directed The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt) have built with their narrative sandbox. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/dark-fantasy-open-world-rpg-the-blood-of-dawnwalker-gets-a-reveal-stream-with-launch-date-next-week-2000137880
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: New Magic: The Gathering Feature Delves Into Final Fantasy XIV Cards' Design Choices
item.content:encoded:
A new feature from the Magic: The Gathering team dives deep into the card mechanic designs of the critically-acclaimed Final Fantasy set—and more specifically, the popular cards based on Final Fantasy 14. 

A card designer responsible for these cards, Cameron Williams, immediately admits he’s a massive FFXIV nerd, which is likely why he landed the role. Therefore, as many FFXIV fans are, he’s very narrative-obsessed in the creation process, and he outlines throughout the balance of that and the actual gameplay implications, both in XIV and MTG. 

A major given example of this is with Emet-Selch; without giving too many spoilers, for the Shadowbringers antagonist, he lays down the foundation of Emet-Selch’s personality and motivations. After they trimmed down some of the text and effects, it leaves him actually flipping between a help and a threat while pursuing his own goal of acting as a herald for things gone, with the draw/discard and graveyard mechanics at play between his two sides. 

As for cards like the Heroes and certain weapons, he talks about how there’s certain obligations to be loyal to how the player approaches XIV. Each of the Hero cards, for example, actually features multiple classes’ weapons, and flavor for the Paladin’s Arms was important enough that it effectively is just a sword and shield (+2/+1). Plus, apparently, Astrologian in FFXIV itself was changed drastically enough during the MTG design process that the designer had to redact certain gameplay aspects. 

Other concepts Williams goes over include Venat and Zodiark, the Wandering Minstrel, the “Primal Garuda” and even Ultima. If you’re a nerd about this side of MTG, this full article is worth perusing, as is the MTG team’s conversation with Tetsuya Nomura. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/new-magic-the-gathering-feature-delves-into-final-fantasy-xiv-cards-design-choices-2000137879
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
